name: Candina Massif and Devil's Eyes Loop
name_original: Solpico - Ojo del Diablo – circular desde Liendo
description: Jog the difficult 7.4-mile Solpico - Eye of the Devil circular route from Liendo, featuring panoramic views and a natural rock window.
region_intro: Jogging around Liendo offers a diverse range of running routes through its varied landscape. The region is characterized by its coastal paths, prominent massifs, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the Cantabrian Sea. Runners can expect terrain that includes both challenging ascents and more level stretches, providing options for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Liendo The most popular jogging route is Ojo del Diablo – circular from Sonabia via the Coastal Path, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete, offering coastal views and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Candina Massif and Devil's Eyes Loop, a difficult 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route features challenging climbs through a massif with panoramic vistas. Local runners also love the Sonabia Beach Viewpoint – Devil's Eye loop from Parque de Sonabia, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through coastal viewpoints and natural rock formations, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging around Liendo is defined by coastal paths, massifs, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more difficult running trails. The routes in Liendo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from around 18 reviews. More than 2800 runners have used komoot to explore Liendo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Liendo?
A: Liendo offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 110 trails to explore. These range from easy jogs to more challenging runs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes in Liendo for beginners?
A: Yes, Liendo has several easier running options. There are 7 routes specifically rated as 'easy', perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed jog. These trails typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Liendo's jogging trails?
A: The jogging trails in Liendo are characterized by a diverse landscape. You'll find coastal paths offering stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea, as well as routes through prominent massifs with challenging ascents and descents. The terrain varies, providing options from level stretches to more rugged paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Liendo?
A: Many of Liendo's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye loop from Oriñón&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, offering a challenging 6.3-mile (10.2 km) run with panoramic views.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Liendo?
A: The running routes in Liendo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from numerous reviews. Runners often praise the breathtaking coastal views, the varied and engaging terrain, and the sense of adventure found on trails through the massifs.

Q: Are Liendo's running trails suitable for families?
A: While many routes in Liendo are rated as difficult due to elevation changes, there are easier options that families can enjoy. Look for the 7 routes specifically categorized as 'easy' for a more family-friendly experience. These trails are generally shorter and less strenuous.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes in Liendo?
A: Most natural trails and paths in Liendo are generally dog-friendly, provided your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Always be mindful of local regulations, particularly around beaches or protected areas, which may have specific rules for dogs.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. Liendo's coastal location means many routes offer spectacular viewpoints. Trails often lead to elevated positions overlooking the Cantabrian Sea, providing stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14997435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Sonabia – Devil's Eye loop from Parque de Sonabia&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features incredible coastal scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Liendo?
A: The best time for jogging in Liendo is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable running conditions. Summers can be warm, especially along exposed coastal paths, while winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Liendo?
A: Yes, Liendo offers several longer running trails for those seeking a greater challenge. Many of the routes through the massifs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998966973/candina-massif-and-devil-s-eyes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candina Massif and Devil's Eyes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, extend for several miles, providing ample distance and elevation for endurance runners.

Q: Are there running routes in Liendo accessible by public transport?
A: While Liendo is a smaller coastal municipality, some routes may be accessible via local bus services connecting to larger towns in Cantabria. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance, as public transport options might be limited, especially for trailheads located outside the main village centers.

Q: Is parking available near the jogging trailheads in Liendo?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the villages and popular starting points for trails in Liendo. Many trailheads, especially those near beaches or natural attractions, often have designated parking areas. However, during peak season, these spots can fill up quickly, so arriving early is recommended.
tips: Summit overlooking the Ojos del Diablo and Laredo

One of the most visited sites in Cantabria, this viewpoint offers majestic panoramic views from the cliff framed by a natural rock window that are worthy of a postcard.
Getting here has its challenging part, but you will enjoy a spectacular and picturesque route with impressive views.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Solpico - Eye of the Devil – circular from Liendo trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult and requires good fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. It involves significant elevation changes and some sections can be very slippery, especially in wet weather. It's best suited for experienced joggers or hikers.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: Given its 11.87 km distance and 449 meters of elevation gain, and its 'difficult' rating, completing this route as a jog can take a couple of hours for very fit individuals. If you're hiking or taking your time to enjoy the views, it could easily take 3-5 hours or more, depending on your pace and breaks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Solpico - Eye of the Devil trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the 'circular from Liendo' variation are not provided, trails to the Devil's Eye in this region often start near villages like Liendo or San Julian Beach. It's advisable to check local signage or komoot's route planner for the most precise starting point and parking options in Liendo.

faqs.question: Is the Solpico - Eye of the Devil – circular from Liendo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information on dog-friendliness for this specific trail is not explicitly stated. However, given the challenging terrain, including rocky and potentially slippery sections, and exposure to the sun, ensure your dog is fit for such conditions. Always keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local regulations and wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Dry conditions are highly preferable for this trail due to potentially very slippery sections in wet weather. Therefore, late spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the best conditions. Be aware that the route can be exposed to the sun for most of its duration, so plan accordingly during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Solpico - Eye of the Devil – circular from Liendo trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: Expect challenging terrain with significant elevation changes. The path can be rocky and bouldery in parts, and some sections may be very slippery, especially if it has rained recently. Much of the route is exposed, offering little shade from the sun.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers spectacular and picturesque panoramic views. Key highlights include the Solpico Summit and the iconic Devil's Eye (Ojo del Diablo), a natural rock window in a cliff that provides stunning vistas of Cantabria.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult terrain and sun exposure, wear sturdy jogging or hiking shoes with good grip. Bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate layers for changing weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Senda del Litoral and the Ruta cima Solpico y Candina. It also briefly touches sections of the Northern Route (Cantabria), Ruta a los Arcos de Llanegro (Ojos del Diablo), and Plano inclinado minero.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, 'Solpico - Eye of the Devil – circular from Liendo' is designed as a circular route, meaning you will end up back near your starting point in Liendo.
